# Give a Nudge

Passive behavioral nudges for Claude Code and Gemini CLI

<!-- ASCII art coming soon — generated by Antigravity -->

> **Terminal sessions only by default.**
> Nudges print to stdout. If you use Claude Code Desktop or a GUI wrapper, nudges won't appear unless you set up desktop notifications (opt-in — see [Desktop Setup](desktop/README.md)).

---

## What it does

Give a Nudge registers hook scripts with Claude Code and Gemini CLI that fire at lifecycle events — session start and tool use by default — and print a single short reminder to your terminal. No dialogs. No required actions. No interruption to flow.

Nudge categories:

- **Context clearing** — fires after 45 minutes of continuous work; prompts `/compact` before quality degrades
- **Usage monitoring** — counts tool calls per session; surfaces a cost-awareness checkpoint every 20 calls
- **Security review** — detects `.env` edits and prompts `/security-audit` at the right moment
- **Command discovery** — once per work session, reminds you which commands explore the codebase, check system state, or run workspace health checks

All behavior is config-driven. Nudges never block, prompt for input, or wait for a response.

---

## Quick Install

Requires `jq` or Python 3. Install one first if needed:

```bash
brew install jq       # macOS
apt install jq        # Debian/Ubuntu
choco install jq      # Windows

python3 --version     # macOS / Linux
python --version      # Windows
```

Then:

```bash
git clone <repo-url>
cd give-a-nudge
bash install.sh
bash tests/nudge-test.sh
```

If `tests/nudge-test.sh` reports all checks passing, the hook installation is working.

Windows users should run the install from Git Bash or WSL so `bash install.sh` works as written.

For step-by-step manual setup without the script, see [install-manual.md](install-manual.md).

---

## Example nudges

What you'll see in your terminal:

```
nudge: .env file edited. Run /security-audit before committing to catch exposed secrets.
nudge: session started. /scout explores your codebase | /health-monitor checks system state | /doctor runs workspace health check.
nudge: long session (45+ min). A fresh context window may improve response quality — consider /compact.
```

---

## How it works

Hook scripts in `core/claude-code/hooks/` and `core/gemini-cli/hooks/` register with each CLI's lifecycle system. When an event fires, the relevant hook reads `nudges.json`, evaluates the trigger condition for that event, checks the cooldown, and prints a message to stdout if the nudge is due.

- **Config-driven**: all nudge definitions live in `nudges.json`. No code changes needed to add, remove, or tune nudges.
- **State tracked** in `~/.claude/nudge-state.json` (Claude Code) or `~/.gemini/nudge-state.json` (Gemini CLI). The state file records last-fired timestamps for cooldown enforcement.
- **Cooldown system**: each nudge has a `cooldown_minutes` field. A nudge that fired 30 minutes ago will not fire again until the cooldown expires.

---

## Customizing nudges

Edit `nudges.json` in your CLI home directory:

- Change `message` to reword any nudge
- Adjust `cooldown_minutes` to fire more or less frequently
- Disable a nudge entirely by removing its entry or setting `"cooldown_minutes": 99999`
- Add a new nudge by following the existing entry format — `id`, `category`, `trigger`, `cooldown_minutes`, `message`

To submit a nudge for others to use, see [community/SUBMIT.md](community/SUBMIT.md).

---

## Desktop Notifications (Optional)

By default, nudges print to stdout and are only visible in terminal sessions. If you run Claude Code Desktop or a GUI wrapper, you can opt in to desktop notifications. The installer already copies the helper scripts for each OS; desktop notifications stay disabled until you enable them.

macOS / Linux:

```bash
export NUDGE_DESKTOP_NOTIFY=true
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('NUDGE_DESKTOP_NOTIFY', 'true', 'User')
```

Then restart your terminal or app session.

Windows can use the built-in PowerShell fallback out of the box. For richer toast notifications, install BurntToast:

```powershell
Install-Module -Name BurntToast -Scope CurrentUser
```

For full platform-specific setup and test commands, see [desktop/README.md](desktop/README.md).

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for full guidelines. There are three paths:

- **New nudge** — open a PR adding your nudge definition to `community/submissions/`
- **Core improvement** — hook logic, state management, installer fixes
- **Docs** — corrections, clarifications, additional examples
